skill.name: php-pro
skill.description: Write idiomatic PHP code with generators, iterators, SPL data structures, and modern OOP features. Use PROACTIVELY for high-performance PHP applications.
skill.summary: Expert PHP development with modern features, generators, SPL structures, and performance optimization.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a prompt-only skill with no executable code. Static analysis scanned 0 files and detected 0 security issues. The skill provides guidance for PHP development without any dangerous patterns, network access, or filesystem operations. Safe for publication.

content.user_title: Master modern PHP development with expert guidance
content.value_statement: Struggling with legacy PHP patterns and inefficient code? This skill delivers memory-efficient generators, SPL data structures, and PHP 8+ features for production-ready applications.
actual_capabilities: Generate memory-efficient code using PHP generators and iterators for large datasets
Implement SPL data structures like SplQueue, SplStack, SplHeap for optimized performance
Apply PHP 8+ features including match expressions, enums, attributes, and constructor property promotion
Enforce strict typing with union types, intersection types, and return type declarations
Design SOLID-compliant architectures with traits, late static binding, and reflection
Profile and optimize PHP applications for production performance
limitations: Cannot execute PHP code or run scripts directly
Cannot install Composer packages or manage dependencies
Cannot access databases or external APIs from within the skill
Relies on user to implement and test the generated code in their environment

use_cases.title: Enterprise PHP Development
use_cases.description: Build scalable, type-safe PHP applications following modern standards and PSR guidelines for enterprise environments.
use_cases.target_user: Backend PHP developers working on large-scale applications

use_cases.title: Performance Optimization
use_cases.description: Refactor memory-intensive code using generators and SPL structures to reduce footprint and improve response times.
use_cases.target_user: Senior developers optimizing high-traffic PHP applications

use_cases.title: PHP Modernization
use_cases.description: Upgrade legacy PHP codebases to PHP 8+ with enums, match expressions, and constructor property promotion.
use_cases.target_user: Development teams modernizing existing PHP projects

prompt_templates.title: Basic PHP Function with Type Safety
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a PHP function that processes an array of user data. Use strict typing, return type declarations, and proper error handling with exceptions.

prompt_templates.title: Generator for Large Dataset Processing
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a PHP generator function that reads and processes a CSV file with millions of rows without loading everything into memory. Include error handling and validation.

prompt_templates.title: SPL Data Structure Implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: Implement a priority queue using SplPriorityQueue for a task scheduler. Include custom task objects with priority levels and deadline handling.

prompt_templates.title: PHP 8 Modern Class with All Features
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a comprehensive PHP 8+ class demonstrating constructor property promotion, enums for status values, attributes for metadata, and match expressions for complex logic. Include interfaces and traits for extensibility.

output_examples.output: A generator-based PHP class that yields processed rows one at a time, with type declarations, custom exceptions for malformed data, and configurable delimiters. Includes iterator implementation for foreach compatibility.

Abstract repository class with generics-like type hints using templates, concrete implementations for specific entities, and interface contracts. Demonstrates late static binding and trait-based caching.

best_practices: Always enable strict_types=1 for predictable type coercion behavior
Use generators for iterating over large datasets to minimize memory usage
Prefer SPL data structures when they provide clear performance or semantic benefits
anti_patterns: Loading entire files or large datasets into arrays before processing
Using array_count_values or similar functions on unbounded data without memory consideration
Mixing business logic with presentation code instead of following separation of concerns

faq.question: What PHP version does this skill target?
faq.answer: This skill focuses on PHP 8.0+ features including enums, match expressions, union types, and constructor property promotion. Code examples work on PHP 8.0 and later versions.

faq.question: Can this skill help with Laravel or Symfony frameworks?
faq.answer: Yes, the core PHP principles apply to all frameworks. While focused on vanilla PHP, the patterns and techniques translate directly to Laravel, Symfony, and other frameworks.

faq.question: Does this skill generate executable code or just guidance?
faq.answer: This skill provides both complete code examples and architectural guidance. All code is production-ready with proper typing, error handling, and documentation.

faq.question: How do generators improve PHP performance?
faq.answer: Generators yield values one at a time instead of building complete arrays in memory. This reduces memory usage from O(n) to O(1) for iteration, crucial for processing large datasets.

faq.question: When should I use SPL data structures over arrays?
faq.answer: Use SPL structures when you need specific behavior: SplQueue for FIFO operations, SplStack for LIFO, SplHeap for priority ordering, or ArrayObject when object-style access is beneficial.

faq.question: Can this skill help with PHP performance profiling?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill provides guidance on identifying bottlenecks using Xdebug, Blackfire, and built-in functions like memory_get_usage(). It also teaches optimization techniques for common performance issues.
